LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D. BOTH MOTIONS ARE CONTESTED. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES) THE COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT WALGREEN CO'S MOTION TO STAY THE PROCEEDINGS. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING.DEFENDANT WALGREEN CO.'S MOTION TO STAY THE ACTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF ALFRED MORALES PENDING A RULING ON CLASS CERTIFICATION IN THE ACTION CAVES ET AL. V. WALGREEN CO., CASE NO. 2:18-CV-02910-MCE-DB, IN THE U.S. DISTRICT COURT FOR THE EASTERN DISTRICT OF CALIFORNIA IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D. BOTH MOTIONS ARE CONTESTED. DEFENDANT ARGUES. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES) THE COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT WALGREEN CO.'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER PRECLUDING PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL FROM COMMUNICATING WITH PUTATIVE CLASS MEMBERS WITHOUT COURT APPROVAL, DIRECTING PRODUCTION OF WEBSITE CONTENTS, AND DIRECTING PRODUCTION OF LIST OF PUTATIVE CLASS MEMBERS THAT CONTACTED PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL FOLLOWING THE MAILING OF COUNSEL'S NEWSLETTER APPEARS TO BE MOOTED IN PART BY THE COURT'S RULING STAYING THIS ACTION. TO THE DEGREE IT IS NOT, IT IS DENIED AS TO ALL THREE REQUESTS. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Defendant Walgreen Co.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Precluding Plaintiffs Counsel From Communicating With Putative Class Members Without Court Approval, Directing Productions Of Website Contents, And Directing Production Of List Of Putative Class Members That Contacted Plaintiffs Counsel Following Plaintiff Counsels Improper Correspondence; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ray Gallo, Esq. 415-257-8800 x11, Dominic Valerian, Esq. 415-423-3573 of Gallo LLP for Plaintiff.
Christopher Archibald, Esq. 949-223-7341 of Bryan Cave Leighton Paisner for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Both motions are contested. Defendant argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT WALGREEN CO's Motion For An Order Precluding Plaintiffs Counsel From Communicating With Putative Class Members Without Court Approval, Directing Productions Of Website Contents, And Directing Production Of List Of Putative Class Members That Contacted Plaintiffs Counsel Following Plaintiff Counsels Improper Correspondence.

Defendant Walgreen Co.'s motion for an order precluding plaintiff's counsel from communicating with putative class members without court approval, directing production of website contents, and directing production of list of putative class members that contacted plaintiff's counsel following the mailing of counsel's newletter appears to be mooted in part by the Court's ruling staying this action. To the degree it is not, it is denied as to all three requests. (see word minutes for full details)(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Defendant Walgreen Co.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay The Proceedings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ray Gallo, Esq. 415-257-8800 x11, Dominic Valerian, Esq. 415-423-3573 of Gallo LLP for Plaintiff.
Christopher Archibald, Esq. 949-223-7341 of Bryan Cave Leighton Paisner for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Both motions are contested. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT WALGREEN CO's Motion To Stay The Proceedings.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Defendant Walgreen Co.'s motion to stay the action filed by Plaintiff Alfred Morales pending a ruling on class certification in the action Caves et al. v. Walgreen Co., Case No. 2:18-cv-02910-MCE-DB, in the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of California is granted. The factors specified in Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. Co. (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, guiding the court's discretion in considering a stay of the state action when there is a pending federal action covering the same subject matter, weigh heavily in favor of staying the instant action.

Morales filed (but did not serve) this action in state court on October 17, 2018. The federal action was filed approximately two weeks later, on November 2, 2018. There are currently two putative class actions pending against the same defendant, asserting substantially similar claims on behalf of nearly identical classes of store managers, and seeking substantially the same relief. In the federal action, significant discovery has been conducted and a briefing schedule set for a hearing on a class certification motion, while the state action has not so advanced. The progress made in the federal court action, filed very close in time after the state action, favors a stay because of the "stage to which the proceedings in the other court have already advanced." (Caiafa Prof. Law Corp., 15 Cal.App.4th at 804.) Further, a stay will avoid the risk of unseemly conflict between the state court and the federal court in ruling on identical issues of law and fact, and will also serve the interests of judicial economy. (Id. at 807.) The fact that the federal action is pending in California rather than in some other state also favors a stay. (Id. at 804.) Plaintiff's choice of forum does not outweigh the interests in avoiding inconsistent rulings by the courts, potential confusion of putative class members, and duplicative efforts by the parties and the courts. "It is undoubtedly preferable from the point of view of the judicial system to resolve the instant dispute by one class action rather than by duplicate class actions in two jurisdictions. If two class actions were allowed to proceed simultaneously, the already overtaxed resources of two courts will have been devoted to the resolution of a single dispute. Judicial economy and efficiency would not be served by allowing both actions to proceed as class actions." (Schneider v. Vennard (1986) 183 Cal.App.3d 1340, 1348 [trial court properly denied class certification on basis of pending federal class action between the parties on the same claims].) (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
